title: Deciphering the single-cell molecular landscape of ampullary cancer: A rare gastrointestinal malignancy.
snippet: Ampullary carcinoma (AC) is a rare epithelial cancer, representing around 0.2% of all gastrointestinal malignancies worldwide, with an estimated incidence of 0.73 per 100,000 people. The rarity of AC, combined with the intricate anatomy of its site of origin, often complicates histopathological diagnosis. Recent advances in molecular research, however, have...
